title: calochlaena straminea
intro: Calochlaena straminea, also known as fern-leaved flax lily or blackboy fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The plant has been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its health benefits also include its ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of Calochlaena straminea can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
